Package com.saperion.common.lang.datatype

Class Binary

java.lang.Object
com.saperion.common.lang.datatype.Binary
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class Binary extends Object implements Serializable

An immutable encapsulation class of binary content into a byte[].

There is no public constructor. Use one of the from...()-factory methods to create a Binary.

An instance of this Binary can be used in several threads concurrently.

Author:
agz
See Also:
  • Serialized Form

    Creates a new Binary with the content of the specified char[] assuming that it consists of base64 encoded quadruples of characters.

    Creates a new Binary with the content of the specified String assuming that it consists of base64 encoded quadruples of characters.

    Creates a new Binary with the content of the specified String section from the specified begin index to the specified end index - 1 assuming that it consists of base64 encoded quadruples of characters.

    Creates a new Binary with the specified content.

    Creates a new Binary with the content of the specified ByteArrayOutputStream.
    static Binary
    fromHexDigits(char[] arHexChars)
    Creates a new Binary with the content of the specified char[] assuming that it consists of pairs of hexadecimal digits optionally separated by other characters as defined by Conversion.convertHexDigitsToByteArray(char[]).

    fromHexDigits(char[] arHexChars, int beginIndex)
    Creates a new Binary with the content of the specified char[] section from the specified begin index to its end assuming that it consists of pairs of hexadecimal digits optionally separated by other characters as defined by Conversion.convertHexDigitsToByteArray(char[], int).

    Creates a new Binary with the content of the specified char[] section from the specified begin index to the specified end index - 1 assuming that it consists of pairs of hexadecimal digits optionally separated by other characters as defined by Conversion.convertHexDigitsToByteArray(char[], int, int).

    Creates a new Binary with the content of the specified String assuming that it consists of pairs of hexadecimal digits optionally separated by other characters as defined by Conversion.convertHexDigitsToByteArray(String).

    Creates a new Binary with the content of the specified String section from the specified begin index to its end assuming that it consists of pairs of hexadecimal digits optionally separated by other characters as defined by Conversion.convertHexDigitsToByteArray(String, int).

  • Method Details

    • fromByteArray

      public static Binary fromByteArray(byte[] arBytes)
      Creates a new Binary with the specified content. The content is copied so that further changes on the specified array will not effect the constructed Binary.

      The specified byte[] must not be null.

      Parameters:
      arBytes - content
      Returns:
      new Binary with the specified content
      See Also:
      • getAsByteArray()
      • fromInputStream(InputStream)

    • getAsByteArray

      public final byte[] getAsByteArray()
      Returns the content of this Binary as a byte[]. The byte[] returned is a copy of the internal content, so that the immutability of this Binary is preserved.
      Returns:
      content as byte[]

    • fromInputStream

      public static Binary fromInputStream(InputStream inputStream) throws IOException
      Creates a new Binary with the content of the specified InputStream.

      The specified InputStream must not be null.

      Parameters:
      inputStream - InputStream to get the content from
      Returns:
      new Binary with content of the specified InputStream
      Throws:
      IOException - on errors during conversion
      See Also:
      • getAsInputStream()
      • fromByteArray(byte[])

    • fromByteArrayOutputStream

      public static Binary fromByteArrayOutputStream(ByteArrayOutputStream byteArrayOutputStream)
      Creates a new Binary with the content of the specified ByteArrayOutputStream.

      The specified ByteArrayOutputStream must not be null.

      Parameters:
      byteArrayOutputStream - ByteArrayOutputStream to get the content from
      Returns:
      new Binary with content of the specified ByteArrayOutputStream
      See Also:
      • fromByteArray(byte[])
      • fromInputStream(InputStream)
      • getAsInputStream()

    • getAsInputStream

      public final InputStream getAsInputStream()
      Returns the content of this Binary as an InputStream.
      Returns:
      content as InputStream

    • fromHexDigits

      public static Binary fromHexDigits(String hexString, int beginIndex, int endIndex)
      Creates a new Binary with the content of the specified String section from the specified begin index to the specified end index - 1 assuming that it consists of pairs of hexadecimal digits optionally separated by other characters as defined by Conversion.convertHexDigitsToByteArray(String, int, int).

      The specified String must not be null.

      Throws an IndexOutOfBoundsException if the specified begin or end index is not inside the specified string or its String.length().

      The specified begin index must not be greater than the specified end index.

      The specified string section must not contain an impair block of hexadecimal digits.

      Parameters:
      hexString - string containing pairwise hexadecimal digits to convert
      beginIndex - begin index of the section of the specified string to convert
      endIndex - end index of the section of the specified string to convert
      Returns:
      new Binary with content of the specified string section
      See Also:
      • getAsHexDigits(String)
      • fromHexDigits(String, int)
      • fromHexDigits(String)
      • fromHexDigits(char[], int, int)

    • fromHexDigits

      public static Binary fromHexDigits(String hexString, int beginIndex)
      Creates a new Binary with the content of the specified String section from the specified begin index to its end assuming that it consists of pairs of hexadecimal digits optionally separated by other characters as defined by Conversion.convertHexDigitsToByteArray(String, int).

      The specified String must not be null.

      Throws an IndexOutOfBoundsException if the specified begin index is not inside the specified string.

      The specified string section must not contain an impair block of hexadecimal digits.

      Parameters:
      hexString - string containing pairwise hexadecimal digits to convert
      beginIndex - begin index of the section of the specified string to convert
      Returns:
      new Binary with content of the specified string section
      See Also:
      • getAsHexDigits(String)
      • fromHexDigits(String, int, int)
      • fromHexDigits(String)
      • fromHexDigits(char[], int)

    • fromHexDigits

      public static Binary fromHexDigits(String hexString)
      Creates a new Binary with the content of the specified String assuming that it consists of pairs of hexadecimal digits optionally separated by other characters as defined by Conversion.convertHexDigitsToByteArray(String).

      The specified String must not be null.

      The specified string must not contain an impair block of hexadecimal digits.

      Parameters:
      hexString - string containing pairwise hexadecimal digits to convert
      Returns:
      new Binary with content of the specified string
      See Also:
      • getAsHexDigits()
      • fromHexDigits(String, int, int)
      • fromHexDigits(String, int)
      • fromHexDigits(char[])

    • fromHexDigits

      public static Binary fromHexDigits(char[] arHexChars, int beginIndex, int endIndex)
      Creates a new Binary with the content of the specified char[] section from the specified begin index to the specified end index - 1 assuming that it consists of pairs of hexadecimal digits optionally separated by other characters as defined by Conversion.convertHexDigitsToByteArray(char[], int, int).

      The specified char[] must not be null.

      Throws an IndexOutOfBoundsException if the specified begin or end index is not inside the specified char[] or equal to its length.

      The specified begin index must not be greater than the specified end index.

      The specified char[] section must not contain an impair block of hexadecimal digits.

      Parameters:
      arHexChars - char[] containing pairwise hexadecimal digits to convert
      beginIndex - begin index of the section of the specified char[] to convert
      endIndex - end index of the section of the specified char[] to convert
      Returns:
      new Binary with content of the specified char[] section
      See Also:
      • getAsHexDigits(String)
      • fromHexDigits(char[], int)
      • fromHexDigits(char[])
      • fromHexDigits(String, int, int)

    • fromHexDigits

      public static Binary fromHexDigits(char[] arHexChars, int beginIndex)
      Creates a new Binary with the content of the specified char[] section from the specified begin index to its end assuming that it consists of pairs of hexadecimal digits optionally separated by other characters as defined by Conversion.convertHexDigitsToByteArray(char[], int).

      The specified char[] must not be null.

      Throws an IndexOutOfBoundsException if the specified begin index is not inside the specified char[].

      The specified char[] section must not contain an impair block of hexadecimal digits.

      Parameters:
      arHexChars - char[] containing pairwise hexadecimal digits to convert
      beginIndex - begin index of the section of the specified char[] to convert
      Returns:
      new Binary with content of the specified char[] section
      See Also:
      • getAsHexDigits(String)
      • fromHexDigits(char[], int, int)
      • fromHexDigits(char[])
      • fromHexDigits(String, int)

    • fromHexDigits

      public static Binary fromHexDigits(char[] arHexChars)
      Creates a new Binary with the content of the specified char[] assuming that it consists of pairs of hexadecimal digits optionally separated by other characters as defined by Conversion.convertHexDigitsToByteArray(char[]).

      The specified char[] must not be null.

      The specified char[] must not contain an impair block of hexadecimal digits.

      Parameters:
      arHexChars - char[] containing pairwise hexadecimal digits to convert
      Returns:
      new Binary with content of the specified char[]
      See Also:
      • getAsHexDigits()
      • fromHexDigits(char[], int, int)
      • fromHexDigits(char[], int)
      • fromHexDigits(String)

    • getAsHexDigits

      public final char[] getAsHexDigits(int beginIndex, int endIndex, String header)
      Returns a char[] containing pairwise hexadecimal digits that represent the bytes of the section of this Binary from the specified begin index to the specified end index - 1 optionally preceded by the specified header string. The resulting char[] has exactly the necessary size.

      Throws an IndexOutOfBoundsException if the specified begin index is not inside this Binary or the specified end index is not inside this Binary or equal to its length.

      The specified begin index must not be greater than the specified end index.

      The specified header is optional (may be null, may be an empty string).

      Parameters:
      beginIndex - begin index of the section of this Binary to convert
      endIndex - end index of the section of this Binary to convert
      header - string to copy at the start of the resulting char[] or null
      Returns:
      new char[] containing pairwise hexadecimal digits that represent the bytes of the section of this Binary from the specified begin index to the specified end index - 1 optionally preceded by the specified header string
      See Also:
      • fromHexDigits(char[], int, int)
      • fromHexDigits(char[], int)
      • fromHexDigits(String, int, int)
      • fromHexDigits(String, int)
      • getAsHexDigits(String)
      • getAsHexDigits()

    • getAsHexDigits

      public final char[] getAsHexDigits(String header)
      Returns a char[] containing pairwise hexadecimal digits that represent the bytes of this Binary optionally preceded by the specified header string. The resulting char[] has exactly the necessary size.

      The specified header is optional (may be null, may be an empty string).

      Parameters:
      header - string to copy at the start of the resulting char[] or null
      Returns:
      new char[] containing pairwise hexadecimal digits that represent the bytes of this Binary optionally preceded by the specified header string
      See Also:
      • fromHexDigits(char[], int, int)
      • fromHexDigits(char[], int)
      • fromHexDigits(String, int, int)
      • fromHexDigits(String, int)
      • getAsHexDigits(int, int, String)
      • getAsHexDigits()

    • getAsHexDigits

      public final char[] getAsHexDigits()
      Returns a char[] containing pairwise hexadecimal digits that represent the bytes of this Binary. The resulting char[] has exactly the necessary size.
      Returns:
      new char[] containing pairwise hexadecimal digits that represent the bytes of this Binary
      See Also:
      • fromHexDigits(char[])
      • fromHexDigits(String)
      • getAsHexDigits(int, int, String)
      • getAsHexDigits(String)

    • fromBase64

      public static Binary fromBase64(String base64String, int beginIndex, int endIndex)
      Creates a new Binary with the content of the specified String section from the specified begin index to the specified end index - 1 assuming that it consists of base64 encoded quadruples of characters.

      The specified String must not be null.

      Throws an IndexOutOfBoundsException if the specified begin or end index is not inside the specified string or its String.length().

      The specified begin index must not be greater than the specified end index.

      The specified string sections size must be a multiple of 4.

      Parameters:
      base64String - string containing base64 encoded quadruples of characters to convert
      beginIndex - begin index of the section of the specified string to convert
      endIndex - end index of the section of the specified string to convert
      Returns:
      new Binary with content of the specified string section
      See Also:
      • getAsBase64()
      • fromBase64(String)
      • fromBase64(char[], int, int)

    • fromBase64

      public static Binary fromBase64(String base64String)
      Creates a new Binary with the content of the specified String assuming that it consists of base64 encoded quadruples of characters.

      The specified String must not be null.

      The specified string size must be a multiple of 4.

      Parameters:
      base64String - string containing base64 encoded quadruples of characters to convert
      Returns:
      new Binary with content of the specified string
      See Also:
      • getAsBase64()
      • fromBase64(String, int, int)
      • fromBase64(char[])

    • fromBase64

      public static Binary fromBase64(char[] arBase64Chars, int beginIndex, int endIndex)
      Creates a new Binary with the content of the specified char[] section from the specified begin index to the specified end index - 1 assuming that it consists of base64 encoded quadruples of characters.

      The specified char[] must not be null.

      Throws an IndexOutOfBoundsException if the specified begin or end index is not inside the specified char[] or equal to its length.

      The specified begin index must not be greater than the specified end index.

      The specified char[] sections size must be a multiple of 4.

      Parameters:
      arBase64Chars - char[] containing base64 encoded quadruples of characters to convert
      beginIndex - begin index of the section of the specified char[] to convert
      endIndex - end index of the section of the specified char[] to convert
      Returns:
      new Binary with content of the specified char[] section
      See Also:
      • getAsBase64()
      • fromBase64(char[])
      • fromBase64(String, int, int)

    • fromBase64

      public static Binary fromBase64(char[] arBase64Chars)
      Creates a new Binary with the content of the specified char[] assuming that it consists of base64 encoded quadruples of characters.

      The specified char[] must not be null.

      The specified char[] size must be a multiple of 4.

      Parameters:
      arBase64Chars - char[] containing base64 encoded quadruples of characters to convert
      Returns:
      new Binary with content of the specified char[]
      See Also:
      • getAsBase64()
      • fromBase64(char[], int, int)
      • fromBase64(String)

    • getAsBase64

      public final char[] getAsBase64(int beginIndex, int endIndex)
      Returns a char[] containing a base64 encoding that represents the bytes of the section of this Binary from the specified begin index to the specified end index - 1. The resulting char[] has exactly the necessary size.

      Throws an IndexOutOfBoundsException if the specified begin index is not inside this Binary or the specified end index is not inside this Binary or equal to its length.

      The specified begin index must not be greater than the specified end index.

      Parameters:
      beginIndex - begin index of the section of this Binary to convert
      endIndex - end index of the section of this Binary to convert
      Returns:
      new char[] containing a base64 encoding that represents the bytes of the section of this Binary from the specified begin index to the specified end index - 1
      See Also:
      • fromBase64(char[], int, int)
      • fromBase64(char[])
      • fromBase64(String, int, int)
      • fromBase64(String)
      • getAsBase64()

    • getAsBase64

      public final char[] getAsBase64()
      Returns a char[] containing a base64 encoding that represents the bytes of this Binary. The resulting char[] has exactly the necessary size.
      Returns:
      new char[] containing a base64 encoding that represents the bytes of this Binary
      See Also:
      • fromBase64(char[])
      • fromBase64(String)
      • getAsBase64(int, int)

      public final int getSize()
      Returns the size of the content of this Binary.

      the size of the content

    • isEmpty

      public final boolean isEmpty()
      Returns whether the content of this Binary is empty.

      whether the content is empty

    • get

      public final byte get(int index)
      Returns the content byte at the specified index.
      Parameters:
      index - index to get the content byte from
      Returns:
      content byte
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if the index is out of bounds
